name: Scottish Oatcakes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup regular oats or quick-cooking oats
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Cut the shortening into the oats, flour, baking soda, and salt until the mixture resembles fine crumbs.
3. Add water, one tablespoon at a time, until the mixture forms a stiff dough.
4. Roll the dough until it is about 1/8 inch thick on a lightly floured surface.
5. Cut into rounds or squares using any cookie cutter available (2-1/2 inches).
6. Place the oatcakes on an ungreased baking sheet.
7. Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the oatcakes start to turn golden brown.
8. Cool on a wire rack.
Note: My oatcakes required several additional minutes before they started turning golden brown (about 20-22 minutes). They remain wonderfully flaky, slightly crunchy, and delicious. It is best to bake them until they are browned rather than following an exact time.
